items: Contract/schema verified
Local fixture behavior verified
Local physical-device behavior verified
Live provider read verified
Live provider write verified when in scope
Production tenancy/policy/egress verified
Human UX/privacy accepted
Release/publishing approved
application_guide_decisions.title: CS-CH-040 required status dimensions

items: Product release v3 remains separate from VS scenario identifiers
CornerStone owns ConnectorPort and Product/Archive code never depends directly on ConnectorHubKit classes or provider SDKs
First live source is read-only GitHub with no Actions and only approved read permissions
Delivery acknowledgement occurs only after durable Artifact/receipt commit
EvidenceRef is metadata; immutable Artifact plus Evidence Bundle remain truth foundations
ConnectorHub captures while CornerStone owns Watch rules, interpretation, approvals, memory, missions, and history
Effective Source Policy intersects organization policy, owner confirmation, workspace classification/retention, product need, provider permissions, and egress
Production tenancy/policy/egress claims require scenario-specific execution evidence before live readiness
Every product capability has a native cornerstone command with JSON output, scope, evidence refs, audit refs, and stable exits
Every Setup Result, Projection, Delivery, Preflight, Result, and Provider Pack is pinned and migration-aware
application_guide_decisions.title: Decisions to freeze before implementation

items: Repository merge or SDK leakage
Acknowledging before archive commit
Treating EvidenceRef as truth
Watch feels like surveillance
Chrome client checks are bypassed
GitHub scope expands into writeback
Fixture success becomes production claim
Prototype runtime copied unchanged
VS2 bypass
Contract/schema drift
application_guide_decisions.title: Risks and mitigations

items: ConnectorHubKit provides a conceptual and local-verification foundation for connected sources
Correct adaptation is a CornerStone-owned adapter and scenario set, not a repo merge or connector-first product
Practical v3 scope is Connected Evidence with selected GitHub read-only plus opt-in WatchAgent macOS/Chrome capture; governed external Actions follow after VS2/action evidence

items: ConnectorHub: Connector / Provider / Action engine concept inside CornerStone
ConnectorHubKit: current Tocktock/Connector-Hub local reference implementation/package
ConnectorPort: proposed CornerStone-owned interface hiding ConnectorHubKit/provider implementation
Connected Source: user-facing CornerStone capability backed by ConnectorHub providers
Projection: normalized provider-derived product input and evidence candidate, not product truth
Delivery: app-scoped transport record carrying a Projection with ack/retry lifecycle
EvidenceRef: safe connector-side provenance metadata, not an immutable Artifact by itself
Setup Result: readiness, mapping, Source Policy, gaps, streams, actions, warnings, and verification result
Source Policy: effective constraints on provider, resources, fields, content, fallback, and raw access
Watch Rule: CornerStone-owned owner-scoped rule defining observations and allowed outputs
Watch Result: Product object separating Observed facts, Inferred meaning, and Proposed next step

application_guide_summary_matrix.source_title: Separate fixture proof from live/production claims

items: GitHub read-only contract pins tenant owner namespace and workspace scope
GitHub read-only contract declares required repository/change reads and optional issue/file reads
Source Policy request selects one repository, metadata/markdown excerpt mode, allowed paths, raw access denied, and retention
Empty actions list is deliberate and must be enforced through schema, Provider Pack manifest, CLI surface, egress policy, and regression tests
Projection-to-Artifact mapping preserves delivery, source, provider, external revision, owner scope, trust state, source policy, and evidence refs
Action handoff envelope carries ActionCard, WorkflowRun, connector preflight, Evidence Bundle, policy, approval, idempotency key, and a CornerStone-derived execution decision

application_guide_verification_plane.title: Native batch verifiers

items: Frozen scenario contract and matrix
Exact source commit/tree and dirty-state metadata
App Requirements/CornerStone contract fixtures and Setup Results
Projection, Delivery, Artifact, Evidence Bundle, Action, and audit records
CLI transcripts with stable exit codes and refs
Browser/UI proof where claimed
Negative-evidence counters
Redaction and secret scans
Provider/network call ledger
Human-required table and human evidence when available
Manifest hashes generated after final report bytes exist

application_guide_verification_plane.title: Required negative-evidence counters

items: CS-CH-H01: real GitHub selected-repository read-only rehearsal
CS-CH-H02: physical macOS permission walkthrough
CS-CH-H03: Chrome permission and privacy review
CS-CH-H04: production-like VS2 integrated security proof
CS-CH-H05: authorized live non-GitHub Action execution
CS-CH-H06: connected-source usability and trust study
CS-CH-H07: backup restore reconciliation and audit recovery exercise
application_guide_verification_plane.title: Human-required gates

items: Phase 0: Contract and adapter foundation verifies CS-CH-001 through CS-CH-006 and CS-CH-038 through CS-CH-040
Phase 1: Durable connected evidence verifies CS-CH-007 through CS-CH-014 and CS-CH-034 to CS-CH-037 using fixtures only
Phase 2: GitHub read-only verifies CS-CH-015 through CS-CH-020 before CH-H01 is attempted
Phase 3: Watch connected evidence verifies CS-CH-021 through CS-CH-028 before CH-H02, CH-H03, and CH-H06
Phase 4: Governed Actions follow corrected VS2 and durable audit proof while GitHub remains read-only
application_guide_verification_plane.title: Recommended implementation sequence

decision_dimension_values.Architecture: implementation stays behind native `cornerstone connector ...` and `cornerstone scenario verify connector-contract-adapter --scenario CS-CH-001` paths, preserving Product / Archive / Connector / Policy / Evidence / Audit boundaries.
decision_dimension_values.Data contracts: the result is bound to matrix row `CS-CH-001`, phase `CH-0`, related requirements `ER-01;ER-02;IR-02;IR-03;IR-04;IR-09`, `proof_surface=local_fixture`, `claim_boundary=deterministic local fixture evidence only; no live-provider production or human-acceptance claim`, and evidence artifact `reports/scenario/connector-contract-adapter/scenarios/CS-CH-001.json` rather than informal assistant confidence.
decision_dimension_values.Domain correctness: the accepted outcome is `Scoped Setup Result is persisted with Source Policy warnings verification refs audit refs and no provider internals`; anything outside that observable behavior remains outside this scenario's PASS claim.
decision_dimension_values.Maintainability: the scenario remains an independent delivery unit in the matrix, report, and verifier so future changes can rerun or update it without depending on another scenario's prose report.
decision_dimension_values.Migration feasibility: the local proof maps to future scoped durable ConnectorHub records with owner, namespace, workspace, evidence, audit, and policy boundaries preserved.
decision_dimension_values.Observability: evidence refs, audit refs, negative counters, filtered scenario reports, and the aggregate connector scenario report are the trace surfaces for review.
decision_dimension_values.Performance: deterministic fixture execution instead of live-provider calls keeps the scenario proof bounded and repeatable; this local PASS makes no production latency, throughput, or scalability claim.
decision_dimension_values.Product value: `CS-CH-001` advances Connector Hub adoption in CornerStone by proving `Register a connector-backed CornerStone capability` as a user-visible connected-source capability inside one CornerStone product, not as a separate ConnectorHub surface.
decision_dimension_values.Reliability: replayable local fixture CLI verification and durable local state serve as the acceptance surface for this independent delivery unit.
decision_dimension_values.Security: provider credentials, raw provider payloads, unauthorized provider calls, live-provider readiness, human-acceptance, and production-readiness claims remain excluded unless explicitly evidenced elsewhere.
decision_dimension_values.Testability: the focused verifier command is `cornerstone scenario verify connector-contract-adapter --scenario CS-CH-001 --json`; the expected method is `Schema unit test plus CLI integration plus durable-state and secret scan`.

decision_dimension_values.Domain correctness: the accepted outcome is `Users see one CornerStone experience with connector internals as admin details`; anything outside that observable behavior remains outside this scenario's PASS claim.
decision_dimension_values.Maintainability: the scenario remains an independent delivery unit in the matrix, report, and verifier so future changes can rerun or update it without depending on another scenario's prose report.
decision_dimension_values.Migration feasibility: the local proof maps to future scoped durable ConnectorHub records with owner, namespace, workspace, evidence, audit, and policy boundaries preserved.
decision_dimension_values.Observability: evidence refs, audit refs, negative counters, filtered scenario reports, and the aggregate connector scenario report are the trace surfaces for review.
decision_dimension_values.Performance: deterministic fixture execution instead of live-provider calls keeps the scenario proof bounded and repeatable; this local PASS makes no production latency, throughput, or scalability claim.
decision_dimension_values.Product value: `CS-CH-039` advances Connector Hub adoption in CornerStone by proving `Present one CornerStone product not a ConnectorHub sub-product` as a user-visible connected-source capability inside one CornerStone product, not as a separate ConnectorHub surface.
decision_dimension_values.Reliability: replayable local fixture CLI verification and durable local state serve as the acceptance surface for this independent delivery unit.
decision_dimension_values.Security: provider credentials, raw provider payloads, unauthorized provider calls, live-provider readiness, human-acceptance, and production-readiness claims remain excluded unless explicitly evidenced elsewhere.
decision_dimension_values.Testability: the focused verifier command is `cornerstone scenario verify connector-contract-adapter --scenario CS-CH-039 --json`; the expected method is `UI navigation copy scan and onboarding test`.

human_gate_operator_handoff_audit.release_impact: Blocks production connected-source and security readiness. Local fixture and current local VS2 proofs remain separate.
human_gate_operator_handoff_audit.stop_or_reject_when: Any cross-namespace read, unexpected egress, missing audit ref, unredacted secret, or unverifiable restore appears.

human_gate_operator_handoff_audit.expected_evidence_summary: Recovery logs, cursor reconciliation, evidence/audit replay.
human_gate_operator_handoff_audit.release_impact: Blocks production operations and recovery readiness. Does not block local fixture ConnectorHub adoption proof.
human_gate_operator_handoff_audit.stop_or_reject_when: Replay changes external state unexpectedly, cursors diverge, audit continuity is broken, or restore evidence is incomplete.

human_gate_operator_handoff_audit.expected_evidence_summary: Redacted permissions, selected repos, calls, Deliveries, audits, zero-write proof.
human_gate_operator_handoff_audit.release_impact: Blocks live GitHub read-only readiness. Does not block local fixture ConnectorHub adoption proof.
human_gate_operator_handoff_audit.stop_or_reject_when: Any write-capable scope is granted, a mutation endpoint is called, repository scope is wider than approved, or audit refs are missing.
human_gate_operator_handoff_audit.why_ai_cannot_verify: Requires a real GitHub account/organization and external permission state.

human_gate_operator_handoff_audit.release_impact: Blocks physical-device Watch and capture readiness. Does not block local fixture capture contract proof.
human_gate_operator_handoff_audit.required_human_action: Grant, pause, revoke, and observe capture.
human_gate_operator_handoff_audit.stop_or_reject_when: Capture starts before consent, pause/revoke is ineffective, status is misleading, or sensitive data is exposed in proof artifacts.
human_gate_operator_handoff_audit.why_ai_cannot_verify: Requires a physical supported Mac and OS permission prompts.

human_gate_operator_handoff_audit.release_impact: Blocks Chrome capture and browser privacy acceptance. Does not block backend fixture policy proof.
human_gate_operator_handoff_audit.required_human_action: Review manual/auto capture, sensitive block, pause/revoke.
human_gate_operator_handoff_audit.stop_or_reject_when: Browser state is captured without clear consent, denied permission is bypassed, degraded mode is unclear, or redaction is insufficient.
human_gate_operator_handoff_audit.why_ai_cannot_verify: Requires real Chrome permission UX and subjective privacy review.

human_gate_operator_handoff_audit.release_impact: Blocks live Action readiness. GitHub remains excluded from this human mutation gate.

human_gate_operator_handoff_audit.stop_or_reject_when: Approval is ambiguous, provider receipt is missing, idempotency cannot be demonstrated, or the action uses GitHub instead of the selected non-GitHub provider.
human_gate_operator_handoff_audit.why_ai_cannot_verify: Mutates an external non-GitHub system and needs authorization.

Required capability missing blocks activation
Optional capability degrades gracefully
Owner confirms or overrides Source Policy

Convert a Projection into an immutable Artifact
Acknowledge only after durable archive commit
Retry transient failures and quarantine poison deliveries
Deduplicate provider events and version changed content
Enforce field and body restrictions from Source Policy
Promote EvidenceRef metadata into an Evidence Bundle
Temporary raw access is denied and tightly bounded
Connect only explicitly selected GitHub repositories
Ingest repository commit change issue and file-snapshot Projections
Incremental GitHub sync is idempotent
Apply GitHub content restrictions and secret hygiene
Deny every GitHub write path
Handle GitHub rate limits revoked permissions and repository removal
macOS capture is off until explicit consent and permission
Turn activity samples into bounded sessions
Create an explicit owner-scoped Watch Rule
Explicit Chrome active-tab capture
Allowlist-based Chrome auto capture with two-sided consent
Block or degrade sensitive page capture
Pause revoke retain export and delete eligible capture state
Separate observation inference and proposal
Combine ActionCard dry-run with ConnectorHub preflight
Require evidence policy and authorized approval
Execute a declared local fixture Action and re-ingest its fixture outcome
Make retries idempotent and expose compensation
Bind every connector app Delivery and Watch to owner and namespace
Enforce default-deny egress around ConnectorHub and tools
Correlate connector audit with CornerStone audit
Version contracts pin Provider Packs and migrate safely

regression_guard_delivery_unit_audit.title: Swap providers without changing Product logic
Untrusted connector content cannot direct agents or actions
Deny undeclared Actions and direct provider bypass
Present one CornerStone product not a ConnectorHub sub-product
Separate fixture proof from live and production claims

remaining_human_external_gate_audit.expected_evidence_summary: Recovery logs, cursor reconciliation, evidence/audit replay.
Redacted permissions, selected repos, calls, Deliveries, audits, zero-write proof.
remaining_human_external_gate_audit.why_ai_cannot_verify: Requires a real GitHub account/organization and external permission state.

remaining_human_external_gate_audit.required_human_action: Grant, pause, revoke, and observe capture.
remaining_human_external_gate_audit.why_ai_cannot_verify: Requires a physical supported Mac and OS permission prompts.

remaining_human_external_gate_audit.required_human_action: Review manual/auto capture, sensitive block, pause/revoke.
remaining_human_external_gate_audit.why_ai_cannot_verify: Requires real Chrome permission UX and subjective privacy review.

remaining_human_external_gate_audit.required_human_action: Approve and execute a reversible live declared Action.
remaining_human_external_gate_audit.why_ai_cannot_verify: Mutates an external non-GitHub system and needs authorization.

remaining_human_external_gate_audit.required_human_action: Complete Connected Sources/Capture Inbox task study.

items: Owner: JiYong / Tars
Recommended product slice: v3 Connected Evidence
Document status: implementation contract candidate ready for owner freeze; product scenarios are not yet verified
source_context_assumptions.title: Document metadata

items: Recommended v3 base is connected evidence rather than a visible Connector Hub product
Smallest complete release sequence runs from capability contract through governed execution outcome re-ingest audit and learning
source_context_assumptions.title: Summary and release sequence

items: Stakeholder conversation and v3 connector/WatchAgent direction
Attached test-scenario implementation prompt and Scenario-First Agent Instruction
Existing ConnectorHub application guide from earlier investigation
Current repository evidence from Connector-Hub and CornerStone architecture/VS2 docs
Older project-sot.md is historical where superseded by current product authority
source_context_assumptions.title: Source basis and authority discipline

items: In scope: contract and Setup Result integration, Source Policy, durable Projection ingestion, Artifact/Evidence handoff, retry/quarantine, GitHub read-only sync, consent-driven macOS/Chrome capture, Watch Rules, Capture Inbox, privacy lifecycle, namespace, audit, and native CLI/API/UI parity

Out of scope before implementation: GitHub mutation, public connector marketplace, production release claims, unbounded autonomy, silent cross-namespace promotion, arbitrary shell/network access, live external writeback in v3 base, broad repository merge, replacing current storage without migration, package publishing, tags, or release operations
source_context_assumptions.title: Frozen implementation scope

items: Product confusion
Domain boundary issues
Validation gaps
Data consistency risks
Downstream read and execution risks
Privacy, security, operational, verification, and claim risks
source_context_assumptions.title: Problem Definition

items: A-01: Product release label v3 is separate from VS-0/VS-1/VS-2 scenario-slice identifiers
A-02: First implementation may use an in-process or local ConnectorHub adapter if process/transport replacement remains possible
A-03: GitHub authentication uses least privilege but exact mechanism is not frozen
A-04: CornerStone archives exact Projection envelope bytes and links provenance; raw provider payloads stay inside ConnectorHub unless separately approved
A-05: First slice may preserve current local runtime storage while defining Postgres/RLS-compatible contracts and migrations
A-06: GitHub remains read-only for the entire program; governed writes apply only to separately approved non-GitHub connectors
A-07: WatchAgent data remains local by default and capture is disabled until explicit user consent plus platform/browser permission
A-08: No new production dependency, production credential, or external mutation is authorized by this document
source_context_assumptions.title: Assumptions

items: Scenario rows are status-neutral acceptance criteria at document generation time
Human-only rows require real accounts, devices, subjective review, or production-like environments
source_context_assumptions.title: Test scenario status rule

Source-control capability family, selected-repository read-only Provider Pack, incremental sync, content restrictions, zero-write guard.
source_implementation_batches.source_release_posture: Live read requires CS-CH-H01; writes remain forbidden.

source_implementation_batches.source_deliverable: macOS consent/permissions, sessionization, Watch Rules, Chrome capture, Capture Inbox, privacy lifecycle, Observation/Inference/Proposal UX.
source_implementation_batches.source_release_posture: Physical-device and browser acceptance require H02/H03/H06.

source_implementation_batches.implementation_phase_name_override_ratio: The implementation document frames CH-4 as a separate governed-action extension, while the application guide narrows the same CS-CH-029 through CS-CH-033 batch to separately approved non-GitHub connector actions after corrected VS2 and durable audit proof. The current phase name preserves that scope boundary without changing scenario coverage or release readiness.
source_implementation_batches.implementation_plan_name: Governed Actions for separately approved connectors
source_implementation_batches.source_deliverable: ActionCard + ConnectorHub preflight, safety-envelope validation, separately approved non-GitHub execution, outcome re-ingest.
source_implementation_batches.source_release_posture: Outside recommended v3 base; blocked by VS2 remediation and H04/H05.

source_initial_scenario_rows.source_evidence: Contract fixture, Setup Result JSON, scoped DB rows, audit refs, zero-secret scan.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_expected_outcome: A scoped Setup Result is persisted with readiness, mappings, Source Policy, warnings, verification refs, and no provider internals.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_situation: An owner is in one tenant/namespace/workspace and submits a versioned connector capability contract.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_trigger: CornerStone validates and registers the contract through its ConnectorPort.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_verification: Schema unit test; CLI/API integration test; durable-state and secret scan.

source_initial_scenario_rows.source_evidence: Blocked Setup Result, non-zero exit code, denial audit, provider-call ledger=0.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_expected_outcome: Activation is blocked with stable reason code, safe resolution guidance, no Delivery stream, and no provider call.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_situation: A contract marks a capability required and no permitted Provider Pack or valid connection can supply it.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_trigger: Setup planning and activation are attempted.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_verification: Invalid/partial contract fixture; activation API/CLI negative test.

source_initial_scenario_rows.source_evidence: Ready-with-gaps record, warning, active streams for available capabilities, regression transcript.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_expected_outcome: Activation succeeds as ready-with-gaps; unavailable UI/actions are disabled without blocking unrelated capabilities.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_situation: Only an optional capability is unavailable.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_trigger: The contract is planned and activated.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_verification: Setup fixture; API and UI degraded-mode test.

source_initial_scenario_rows.source_evidence: Before/after Source Policy snapshots, policy decision, owner identity, audit event.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_expected_outcome: A new immutable Source Policy snapshot is stored, constraints never broaden silently, and the change is audited.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_situation: Setup recommends one provider/source policy and the owner has authority to confirm or override it.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_trigger: The owner confirms or selects another declared compatible source.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_verification: Policy normalization unit test; API/UI owner action test; state diff.

source_initial_scenario_rows.source_evidence: Same product object output, changed provider refs, unchanged handler code hash, audit diff.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_expected_outcome: Product handlers and downstream domain logic continue unchanged; only setup/source references change.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_situation: Two Provider Packs expose the same Common Capability and Projection contract.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_trigger: Source Policy switches the selected provider.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_verification: Provider-swap integration test against common fixtures.

source_initial_scenario_rows.source_evidence: Redacted status payload, reason code, resolution steps, secret/path scan=0 findings.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_expected_outcome: CornerStone explains cause, impact, and safe resolution without exposing tokens, paths, handles, or raw provider responses.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_situation: A credential, scope, platform permission, or selected-resource gap exists.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_trigger: The user opens setup/status surfaces.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_verification: Error-shape unit tests; CLI/API/UI snapshot; secret fixtures.

source_initial_scenario_rows.source_evidence: Artifact checksum, stored envelope bytes, provenance links, scope fields, audit event.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_expected_outcome: The exact envelope is preserved as an immutable scoped Artifact/intake record, linked to Projection, Source Policy, EvidenceRef, and connector audit refs.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_situation: A valid app-scoped Projection Delivery arrives.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_trigger: The Connector ingress handler processes it.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_verification: Integration test with fixture delivery; database/object-store checks.

source_initial_scenario_rows.source_evidence: Crash transcripts, DB rows, unique-key result, ack timeline, acknowledged_without_artifact=0.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_expected_outcome: No acknowledgement occurs before durable commit; redelivery yields one logical Artifact and acknowledgement only after transaction success.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_situation: A Delivery can be redelivered and the process may fail between receipt and commit.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_trigger: The handler crashes before commit or after commit but before acknowledgement.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_verification: Fault-injection integration test at transaction boundaries.

source_initial_scenario_rows.source_evidence: Attempt counts, retry schedule, quarantine item, redacted error, audit events.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_expected_outcome: Transient failures retry with bounds/backoff; poison deliveries enter quarantine with safe diagnostics; unrelated streams continue.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_situation: A handler or provider fails transiently, repeatedly, or with malformed payload.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_trigger: Delivery retry policy is applied.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_verification: Retry clock test; malformed payload integration test; queue state check.

source_initial_scenario_rows.source_evidence: Unique dedupe key, content hashes, version lineage, no duplicate search truth.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_expected_outcome: Duplicates resolve to one logical intake record; changed content creates a version linked to its predecessor and source revision.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_situation: The same provider event or unchanged source content is received multiple times, then later changes.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_trigger: Deliveries are processed.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_verification: Dedupe and one-byte/change fixture tests; lineage query.

source_initial_scenario_rows.source_evidence: Normalized Projection, excluded-field list, policy decision, raw-content persistence=0.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_expected_outcome: Disallowed fields are rejected or stripped before durable Product state; a policy decision and helpful error are recorded.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_situation: Source Policy limits metadata/body fields, content size, selected resources, paths, or raw access.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_trigger: A Projection arrives with fields beyond the allowed contract.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_verification: Property-based validator tests; oversized/extra-field fixtures.

source_initial_scenario_rows.source_evidence: Evidence Bundle JSON, artifact checksum, connector refs, claim trust state, audit refs.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_expected_outcome: CornerStone assembles an Evidence Bundle containing Artifact, Delivery, Setup Result, Source Policy, EvidenceRef, query/snapshot, and audit refs; EvidenceRef alone is never treated as the original.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_situation: An Artifact was created from a Projection and ConnectorHub supplied EvidenceRef metadata.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_trigger: A brief, claim, or action requests evidence.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_verification: Evidence assembly unit/integration test; claim creation test.

source_initial_scenario_rows.source_evidence: Denied/granted decisions, expiry proof, read counter, revoked handle, no handle in logs.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_expected_outcome: Default request is denied; an allowed grant is purpose-bound, expiring, counted, redacted, scoped, and revocable.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_situation: Raw access is absent from the contract or declared with strict TTL/read limits.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_trigger: An app or operator requests raw evidence.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_verification: Denial test; TTL/max-read boundary tests; log/UI scan.

source_initial_scenario_rows.source_evidence: Untrusted label, blocked tool/action records, external calls=0, audit/policy evidence.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_expected_outcome: Content remains untrusted evidence; no authority changes, tool calls, connector actions, or external egress are triggered.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_situation: Connector content includes instructions to ignore policy, reveal secrets, or trigger a tool/action.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_trigger: The Projection is archived, summarized, searched, or used by an agent.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_verification: Prompt-injection fixture through ingest/search/agent/action paths.

source_initial_scenario_rows.source_evidence: Installation scope, selected repo list, denied access result, provider-call ledger.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_expected_outcome: Only explicitly selected repositories are visible and ingestible; organization-wide or unselected repository access is denied.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_situation: A GitHub read-only connector is installed for selected repositories.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_trigger: The owner chooses repositories and activates sync.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_verification: Selected-resource integration test with allowed and denied repo fixtures.

source_initial_scenario_rows.source_evidence: Projection fixtures, Artifact records, source refs, search snapshot, Evidence Bundle.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_expected_outcome: Versioned source-control Projections map into immutable Artifacts and searchable evidence with source revision and repository provenance.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_situation: A selected repository contains repository metadata, commits, pull requests, issues, and allowed file snapshots.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_trigger: Initial or incremental read sync runs.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_verification: Fixture sync integration tests per Projection type; search/evidence tests.

source_initial_scenario_rows.source_evidence: Cursor snapshots, dedupe records, reconciliation report, no duplicate Artifact truth.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_expected_outcome: Provider event IDs, cursors, source revisions, and content hashes produce idempotent ordered state without missed or duplicated logical content.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_situation: Polling and webhook signals may overlap, arrive out of order, or repeat.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_trigger: Incremental GitHub synchronization processes them.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_verification: Out-of-order/replay/cursor integration tests.

source_initial_scenario_rows.source_evidence: Decision records, redacted Artifact, skipped/quarantine state, secret scan=0.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_expected_outcome: Policy skips, truncates, redacts, or quarantines content according to path, size, type, and secret rules; source remains read-only.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_situation: Repository content may be large, binary, secret-bearing, generated, or outside allowed paths.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_trigger: The GitHub mapper evaluates the content.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_verification: Boundary fixtures for bytes, binary type, secret markers, paths, and prompt injection.

source_initial_scenario_rows.source_evidence: actions=[] contract, absence scan, denied result, github_write_calls=0.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_expected_outcome: The operation is denied before network execution; no GitHub write capability, action schema, token scope, route, or UI control exists.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_situation: Any Product, agent, pack, CLI, API, or provider path attempts a GitHub mutation.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_trigger: A write-like operation is requested or an undeclared endpoint is reached.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_verification: Static capability/permission scan; API/CLI negative tests; controlled egress ledger.
source_initial_scenario_rows.type_escalation_override_rationale: GitHub write denial is a release-blocking safety guarantee for the read-only GitHub source slice, so the current CornerStone matrix elevates the source regression framing to MUST_PASS while preserving the local-fixture-only claim boundary.

source_initial_scenario_rows.source_evidence: Rate-limit record, retry time, revoked/removed state, freshness marker, audit events.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_expected_outcome: CornerStone preserves existing evidence, reports freshness/setup gaps, retries safely when appropriate, stops removed scopes, and never fabricates current data.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_situation: GitHub rate limits, revoked installation permission, repository removal, or transient transport errors occur.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_trigger: Sync or status runs.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_verification: Failure simulations and stale-data API/UI tests.

source_initial_scenario_rows.source_evidence: Consent record, permission state, capture_before_consent=0, status/audit output.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_expected_outcome: No activity is captured until explicit consent and required platform permissions exist; unsupported/missing states are explained safely.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_situation: WatchAgent is installed on a supported or unsupported host without consent/permission.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_trigger: Collection starts or status is checked.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_verification: Fixture unit tests plus supported-host integration harness; physical proof deferred.

source_initial_scenario_rows.source_evidence: Input samples, resulting sessions, confidence/caveats, dedupe and retention records.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_expected_outcome: Samples become bounded ActivitySession Projections with deterministic boundaries, confidence, caveats, and no unsupported intent claim.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_situation: Permissioned activity samples include app switches, idle gaps, duplicates, and low-information noise.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_trigger: Sessionization runs.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_verification: Deterministic timeline unit tests with boundary values and idle windows.

source_initial_scenario_rows.source_evidence: WatchRule versions, policy snapshot, owner action, audit timeline.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_expected_outcome: The rule is explicit, scoped, source-limited, reviewable, audited, and cannot broaden capture without a new confirmation.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_situation: An owner wants monitoring for a project, source, condition, and time window.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_trigger: The owner creates, pauses, resumes, edits, or deletes a Watch Rule.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_verification: Domain/API/UI lifecycle tests; cross-scope denial tests.

source_initial_scenario_rows.source_evidence: Permission event, payload, backend decision, summary/evidence, raw_text/html_stored=false.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_expected_outcome: Only the active page is considered; policy is rechecked server-side; summary/evidence is created or safely blocked without storing raw HTML/text.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_situation: The Chrome extension is loaded and the user invokes capture on the active tab.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_trigger: The extension requests temporary active-tab access and submits a bounded payload to the local backend.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_verification: Extension unit tests, mocked Chrome API tests, backend contract tests, manual browser proof.

source_initial_scenario_rows.source_evidence: Consent and config snapshots, permission status, history/timeline, unapproved_domain_captures=0.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_expected_outcome: Capture occurs only on an active allowed page within throttle/session limits; consent/config mismatch or missing permission blocks it.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_situation: Auto capture is configured for approved domains/source packs with browser permission and matching backend consent/config versions.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_trigger: A page-load, URL-change, or tab-activate trigger occurs.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_verification: Extension state-machine tests; backend consent/config/idempotency tests.

source_initial_scenario_rows.source_evidence: Decision code, degraded payload, history item, raw persistence=0, UI explanation.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_expected_outcome: The page is blocked or degraded to domain/hash-only metadata; no forbidden content is sent or stored, and the reason is visible.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_situation: A page is sensitive, contains password/payment/secret fields, is browser-internal/private, or is an unknown editable surface.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_trigger: Capture policy evaluates it.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_verification: Policy unit tests and browser/backend fixtures for every sensitive class.

source_initial_scenario_rows.source_evidence: Pause/revoke timestamps, export bundle, deletion receipt, retained-audit explanation.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_expected_outcome: Collection stops promptly; decisions persist; exports are scoped/redacted; deletion follows retention/audit policy and is explained.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_situation: A user has collected WatchAgent/Chrome state.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_trigger: The user pauses/revokes sources, changes retention, exports data, dismisses/saves results, or deletes eligible local state.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_verification: Lifecycle API/UI tests; state and file checks; retention boundary tests.

source_initial_scenario_rows.source_evidence: WatchResult JSON, evidence refs, confidence/caveats, trust state, UI proof.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_expected_outcome: The result visibly separates Observation, Inference, Evidence/Caveats, and Proposed Action or Memory; unsupported inference remains Draft/Hypothesis.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_situation: One or more captured signals support a potential interpretation and next step.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_trigger: CornerStone creates a Watch Result.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_verification: Domain mapping and UI snapshot/browser test; evidence coverage check.

source_initial_scenario_rows.source_evidence: ActionCard, preflight record, policy input, expected calls, real calls=0, audit refs.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_expected_outcome: One combined review shows diff/impact, provider support, permissions, Source Policy, risk, idempotency, expected calls, evidence, and approval need; no side effect occurs.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_situation: A separately approved non-GitHub connector action is proposed from a claim or mission.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_trigger: CornerStone runs ActionCard dry-run and ConnectorHub Action Preflight.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_verification: Dry-run/preflight integration test; call ledger assertion.

source_initial_scenario_rows.source_evidence: Denial decisions, zero external calls, audit events, stable exit codes.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_expected_outcome: Execution is denied with a precise cause and resolution; ConnectorHub cannot infer product approval and Product cannot infer connector permission.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_situation: A side-effecting connector action lacks evidence, policy allow, authorized approval, required permission, or idempotency key.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_trigger: Execution is requested.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_verification: Combinatorial negative tests for each missing gate.

source_initial_scenario_rows.source_evidence: Execution request/result, idempotency record, provider receipt, outcome Artifact/Evidence Bundle, audit chain.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_expected_outcome: An Action Result is linked to WorkflowRun and audit, external outcome is re-ingested as evidence, and no duplicate side effect occurs.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_situation: All gates pass for a declared, separately approved non-GitHub action.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_trigger: WorkflowRun invokes ConnectorHub execution once.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_verification: Fixture integration test; live human gate for real provider.

source_initial_scenario_rows.source_evidence: Denied response, audit event, provider calls=0, credential exposure=0.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_expected_outcome: Backend enforcement denies it, records the attempt, and exposes no provider client or secret even if UI checks are bypassed.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_situation: An agent, Product module, or extension attempts an undeclared action or direct provider/credential access.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_trigger: The bypass path is invoked.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_verification: Static dependency scan; direct-call negative tests; sandbox/egress test.

source_initial_scenario_rows.source_evidence: Single provider effect, existing result linkage, conflict denial, compensation plan.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_expected_outcome: Same key returns the existing result; conflicting intent is rejected; compensation/rollback expectation is visible when atomic rollback is impossible.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_situation: A side-effecting Action is retried because of timeout, duplicate request, or ambiguous provider response.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_trigger: The same or conflicting idempotency key is submitted.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_verification: Idempotency concurrency tests; timeout/ambiguous-result simulation.

source_initial_scenario_rows.source_evidence: Denied cross-scope reads/actions, scoped rows, policy decisions, leak counter=0.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_expected_outcome: All objects and results remain scoped; no ownerless or cross-namespace Delivery, evidence, memory, or action is returned or executed.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_situation: Two owners/workspaces use connector capabilities with similar external IDs.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_trigger: They register, poll, search, watch, or execute.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_verification: Cross-tenant/namespace integration matrix and DB/RLS tests.

source_initial_scenario_rows.source_evidence: Scan report=0 findings, rotation/revocation event, safe connection status.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_expected_outcome: Only credential references/fingerprints/status are visible; raw secrets and handles never cross the ConnectorHub boundary.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_situation: Provider credentials exist or rotate/revoke.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_trigger: Product, logs, reports, UI, exports, errors, and durable state are inspected.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_verification: Seeded-secret scan over API/CLI/UI/log/state/export artifacts.

source_initial_scenario_rows.source_evidence: Blocked sink traces, policy decision, DNS/redirect/proxy/subprocess results, egress calls=0.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_expected_outcome: Default-deny egress blocks the call, logs policy reason, and no alternate route bypasses the gateway.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_situation: ConnectorHub, a tool runtime, or untrusted content attempts an external call outside an explicitly allowed provider route.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_trigger: Network access is attempted, including redirects, alternate addresses, proxy, subprocess, or protocol variants.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_verification: Production-like network integration suite; local unit mocks are insufficient for final PASS.

source_initial_scenario_rows.source_evidence: Connector event, CornerStone event, correlation ID, object refs, integrity verification output.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_expected_outcome: Connector event IDs correlate to CornerStone audit events and affected objects without copying secrets/raw payloads; integrity verification still works.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_situation: Connector setup, delivery, evidence access, policy, action, retry, quarantine, or credential events occur.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_trigger: CornerStone records the corresponding lifecycle.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_verification: Audit contract tests; correlation query; tamper test.

source_initial_scenario_rows.source_evidence: Version pins, diff, migration record, canary result, rollback transcript.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_expected_outcome: Pinned versions remain active until reviewed; diff/compatibility/migration plan is shown; incompatible changes block activation; rollback is available.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_situation: A contract, Projection schema, SDK protocol, or Provider Pack version changes.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_trigger: A workspace plans or applies an upgrade.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_verification: Contract compatibility fixtures; upgrade/downgrade migration tests.

source_initial_scenario_rows.source_evidence: First-value transcript without connector setup, connected-source walkthrough, forbidden-name scan.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_expected_outcome: The user sees one CornerStone product and plain product concepts; repo/package names and connector internals remain admin details.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_situation: Connector capabilities are added to navigation, onboarding, CLI, and help.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_trigger: A normal user completes first value and connected-source setup.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_verification: UI/navigation copy scan; onboarding browser test; human UX gate.

source_initial_scenario_rows.requirement_enrichment_override_rational: CS-CH-040 is the report-lint guard that keeps the ConnectorHub evidence package engineer-executable: it forces scenario reports to preserve row statuses, human-required gates, exact evidence, negative overclaim counters, and weakest-verdict boundaries instead of becoming a descriptive summary.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_evidence: Scenario statuses, human-required table, negative overclaim counter, exact commit/tree metadata.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_expected_outcome: The report labels fixture/local evidence accurately, keeps live/production items unverified or human-required, and never upgrades readiness by implication.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_situation: Fixture tests pass but live credentials, physical devices, human review, or production topology are absent.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_trigger: A report or release claim is generated.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_verification: Report-lint tests and evidence manifest review.

source_initial_scenario_rows.source_evidence: Redacted permissions, selected repos, call ledger, Deliveries, audit refs, zero-write proof.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_expected_outcome: Read Projections and audit evidence are produced for selected repos, and an independent call/permission review shows zero write capability or write calls.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_situation: A real GitHub account/organization and least-privilege installation are available.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_trigger: An authorized owner installs the connector for selected repositories and runs a read-only rehearsal.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_verification: Human/external account rehearsal.

source_initial_scenario_rows.source_evidence: Recording/screenshots, status transcript, first sample, pause/revoke timestamps.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_expected_outcome: Capture follows visible consent and permission state and stops on pause/revoke without unexpected observation.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_situation: A supported physical Mac is available.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_trigger: A human grants, pauses, revokes, and observes activity permission behavior.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_verification: Physical-device walkthrough.

source_initial_scenario_rows.source_evidence: Recording/screenshots, permission pages, timeline, issues, accept/reject note.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_expected_outcome: The permission/privacy experience is understandable and behavior matches the contract.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_situation: Chrome extension and local backend are installed in a real browser profile.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_trigger: A human reviews setup, active-tab capture, allowlist auto capture, sensitive blocking, pause, and revoke.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_verification: Human browser/privacy review.

source_initial_scenario_rows.source_evidence: Scenario-specific DB/policy/network transcripts, reports, backup/restore evidence.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_expected_outcome: Connector operations enforce scope, policy, egress, backup/restore, and audit under real topology.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_situation: An integrated production-like environment with trusted RequestContext, PostgreSQL/RLS, OPA, and network controls exists.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_trigger: The corrected security/tenancy/egress scenario suite runs end to end.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_verification: Human-authorized production-like verification.

source_initial_scenario_rows.source_evidence: Approval, redacted request/result, provider receipt/state, idempotency and audit evidence.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_expected_outcome: The provider state changes exactly once and all safety-envelope, result, compensation, and audit evidence is captured.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_situation: A separately approved non-GitHub provider and reversible test target exist.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_trigger: An authorized reviewer approves and executes one live declared Action.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_verification: Human-authorized external mutation.

source_initial_scenario_rows.source_evidence: Timed task, notes, recording/screenshots, acceptance decision and issue list.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_expected_outcome: The flow is understandable, trustworthy, and does not require knowledge of internal repositories or connector architecture.
source_initial_scenario_rows.source_situation: A representative user can use the integrated CornerStone UI.

source_verification_plan.title: Automatically verifiable items

items: No ownerless connector rows
One logical processed record per scoped provider event/idempotency key
Every acknowledged Delivery references a committed immutable Artifact/intake record
Every connector-derived Evidence Bundle links its Artifact, Delivery, Source Policy, Setup Result, and EvidenceRef metadata
No credentials, raw local paths, provider raw payload markers, or raw access handles appear in durable Product state
GitHub contract and Provider Pack contain no write Action or write permission
Watch capture records have consent/config/policy references and raw_text_stored=false/raw_html_stored=false where required
source_verification_plan.title: Proposed database and state checks

items: Frozen scenario contract/matrix and exact source commit/tree/dirty state
Contract fixture, Setup Result, Source Policy, Projection, Delivery, Artifact, Evidence Bundle, and relevant action/audit records
CLI/API transcripts with stable exit codes and object/evidence/audit/policy references
Database/object-store state checks and hash manifest
UI/browser proof only for rows claiming UI behavior
Secret/redaction scan, provider/network call ledger, and negative-evidence counters
Failure reverse-engineering record for FAIL/NOT_VERIFIED/NOT_RUN rows
Human evidence attached only to HUMAN_REQUIRED rows

production_readiness_overclaims

source_verification_plan.title: Human-required checks

items: Live GitHub and browser/device claims until H01-H03 evidence exists
Production scope/policy/egress/recovery claims until corrected VS2 and H04/H07 evidence exists
Live side-effecting Action claims until H05 evidence exists
Human trust/usability claims until H06 evidence exists
source_verification_plan.title: Not-yet-verifiable items

items: Every applicable MUST_PASS row is PASS with concrete scenario-specific evidence
Every applicable REGRESSION row is PASS with concrete evidence
No AI-verifiable row is FAIL, NOT_VERIFIED, or NOT_RUN
Every HUMAN_REQUIRED row states required action, expected evidence, and release impact; release claims do not exceed completed human gates
Native CLI/API/UI parity exists for each claimed Product feature
Evidence package contains exact source state, transcripts, object/state checks, audit refs, negative counters, and failure analysis
GitHub write capability and calls remain zero
Production readiness is not claimed until VS2 scenario-specific security evidence and required human gates are complete
Final verdict equals the weakest applicable scenario result
